senses.definition: being optimistic and behaving in a cheerful manner

examples.x: Despite the challenges they faced, her buoyant spirit kept the team motivated and hopeful.
His buoyant attitude was infectious, lifting the mood of everyone around him.
After receiving the good news, she felt buoyant and full of energy for the rest of the day.
The children were buoyant with excitement as they prepared for their first trip to the amusement park.
His buoyant personality made him popular among his peers and a joy to work with.

senses.definition: filled with a sense of joy or happiness, often to the point of appearing to glow
having a positive, cheerful, or optimistic attitude or mood

examples.x: Her upheat personality made her a favorite among her coworkers, always bringing positivity to the office.
The festival was filled with upheat music and joyful crowds, creating an atmosphere of celebration.

senses.definition: having a lively and enthusiastic quality

examples.x: Her bubbly personality brought a burst of energy to the dull office environment.
Despite the rainy weather, her bubbly enthusiasm for the outdoor event was contagious.

senses.definition: excited and happy because something has happened or is going to happen

examples.x: She was elated to receive the news of her promotion at work.
He was elated to be accepted into his dream college.
Winning the competition left her feeling elated and grateful.
The team was elated after winning the championship game.
She was elated when she found out she was going to be a parent.

senses.definition: experiencing a state of perfect happiness

examples.x: After a long day of relaxation, she felt blissful lying in a hammock, watching the sunset.
The couple exchanged blissful smiles as they celebrated their anniversary in a romantic setting.
The blissful melody of the song transported listeners to a world of tranquility and happiness.
The blissful expression on his face revealed the joy of achieving a long-sought goal.
The aroma of freshly baked cookies filled the kitchen, creating a blissful homey atmosphere.

senses.definition: experiencing or expressing extreme happiness

examples.x: The children were jubilant on the last day of school, shouting and laughing with delight.
The community was jubilant during the festive parade, celebrating with music, dance, and colorful decorations.
After receiving the scholarship, Maria was jubilant, expressing her joy with hugs and grateful smiles.
The fans became jubilant when their favorite team scored the winning goal in the final minutes of the match.
The surprise birthday party left Emily jubilant, surrounded by friends and family expressing their love and good wishes.

senses.definition: having a cheerful and friendly demeanor

examples.x: His jovial nature and infectious laughter made him the life of every party.
The restaurant's jovial staff contributed to a delightful dining experience for the patrons.
Even in stressful situations, her jovial attitude helped ease tension and foster collaboration.
The jovial group of friends made the road trip a memorable adventure filled with laughter.
The jovial atmosphere at the family reunion was marked by laughter, games, and shared stories.

senses.definition: unable to stay still and calm

examples.x: During the boring lecture, the students grew increasingly fidgety, glancing at the clock every few minutes.

senses.definition: having a nervous or restless energy

examples.x: The jittery feeling before a big presentation often accompanies increased heart rate and sweaty palms.
The jittery anticipation of the exam results kept the students on edge for days.
The loud noise unexpectedly startled her, making her feel jittery and anxious.
The audience grew jittery as the performance time approached.
He felt jittery before meeting his new boss.

senses.definition: feeling abandoned or hopeless

examples.x: The abandoned puppy looked forlorn, waiting by the roadside for its owner to return.
She felt forlorn after her friends canceled their plans at the last minute.
He appeared forlorn as he wandered the empty streets late at night.
They became forlorn when their hopes of finding a job were repeatedly dashed.
She looked forlorn sitting by herself at the park, watching others enjoy their company.

senses.definition: feeling or displaying deep exhaustion

examples.x: After a long day of hiking, he felt weary and ready to rest.
The weary travelers finally reached their destination after a grueling journey.
Her weary eyes betrayed the toll of sleepless nights and demanding work.
After a long week of work, she felt weary and in need of a break.
The weary students struggled to stay focused during the last lecture of the day.

senses.definition: experiencing extreme exhaustion, often resulting from physical or mental exertion

examples.x: After working three consecutive night shifts, she felt completely fatigued and could barely keep her eyes open.
The long hike up the mountain left him feeling fatigued, and he needed to rest for a while.
Driving for hours on end made him feel fatigued, so he pulled over to take a nap.
The emotional strain of dealing with the loss of a loved one left her mentally fatigued and drained.

senses.definition: depleted of physical or emotional energy
dissatisfied or unhappy due to feelings of unfair treatment or disappointment

examples.x: The disgruntled employee expressed frustration with the company's new policies during the meeting.
After receiving cold and unhelpful customer service, the disgruntled customer decided to switch to a different provider.
The disgruntled passengers complained about the delayed flight and lack of communication from the airline staff.
The disgruntled residents gathered at the town hall to voice their concerns about the ongoing construction noise.
Feeling overlooked for a promotion, the disgruntled worker considered looking for a new job.

senses.definition: feeling intense frustration, especially due to an unsolvable problem

examples.x: She let out an exasperated sigh when she realized she had forgotten her keys again.
Her exasperated tone indicated that she had reached her limit with the situation.

senses.definition: irritated or angered by a particular situation or person
deeply troubled, disappointed, or baffled, often as a result of an unexpected or unfavorable event

examples.x: Dismayed tourists faced disrupted vacation plans due to a sudden airline strike.
Dismayed hikers found the trail closed due to unforeseen weather conditions.

senses.definition: lazy and dreamy, without much energy or interest

examples.x: On hot summer days, she felt lackadaisical and preferred lying on the hammock over any activity.
The melody of the slow song put her in a lackadaisical mood, making her drift into her own world.
His lackadaisical attitude towards his studies resulted in poor grades.
The lackadaisical employee's work ethic was causing frustration among his colleagues.
She approached the project with a lackadaisical mindset, resulting in delays and errors.

senses.definition: feeling hopeless, discouraged, or in low spirits, often due to a sense of failure or loss

examples.x: The failure of his business left him feeling despondent and unsure about the future.
The despondent look on her face revealed the emotional toll of the recent breakup.
The despondent atmosphere in the office was palpable after news of the company's downsizing.
The despondent community struggled to recover after the natural disaster devastated their homes.
She became despondent after receiving the rejection letter from her dream university.
A despondent expression crossed her face when she saw the empty room.

senses.definition: having lost all one's courage, hope, or enthusiasm

examples.x: The repeated setbacks in his career left him feeling disheartened and uncertain about his future.
The disheartened volunteers faced challenges in maintaining enthusiasm for their community project.
The disheartened athlete struggled to regain confidence after a series of disappointing performances.

senses.definition: feeling downcast, discouraged, or in low spirits

examples.x: His dejected expression revealed the disappointment of not getting the promotion.
The dejected crowd slowly dispersed after the cancellation of the outdoor event due to bad weather.
The dejected student struggled to hide his disappointment upon seeing the exam results.

senses.definition: (of a person or their manner) melancholic and full of grief

examples.x: She walked with downcast eyes, lost in her thoughts of sorrow.

senses.definition: feeling disappointed and sad, especially due to experiencing an unexpected failure

examples.x: He looked crestfallen when he realized he had missed the deadline for submitting his application.
She looked crestfallen as she watched her dream of studying abroad slip away due to financial constraints.
They were crestfallen when their project, on which they had worked tirelessly, was rejected.
He felt crestfallen after learning that he hadn't been selected for the lead role in the play.
She became crestfallen upon discovering that her artwork had been vandalized.

senses.definition: filled with lively energy and excitement

examples.x: The children's exuberant laughter echoed throughout the park as they played.
At the concert, the band's exuberant performance had everyone on their feet, singing along.
Her exuberant personality lit up the room, bringing smiles to everyone's faces.
The fans were exuberant as they cheered for their favorite team at the championship game.
The exuberant puppy bounded around the yard, chasing after anything that moved.

senses.definition: tremendously excited about something

examples.x: After winning the championship, she was on cloud nine.
Getting engaged to the love of her life put her on cloud nine.
He couldn't stop smiling after his surprise birthday party; he was on cloud nine the entire evening.
Receiving the acceptance letter to her dream university put her on cloud nine.
